calibers.descr: This file is generated from mixed sources.  Chamber from a Manson / Reyneke print.  Cartridge by guess work reducing some dimensions just enough to allow the case to chamber.  Manson prints are a mix of SAAMI and CIP parameters, thus making exact duplication a bit difficult.  The drawing specifies a .075" freebore but also says freebore is to allow a 75 ELD-M bullet to be forward of the neck/shoulder junction.  .075" does not seem long enough to provide this requirement, so it is assumed the freebore is to be established after the chamber is reamed by the use of a separate throating reamer.

From JGS reamer print, no cartridge print supplied.I made the cartridge L1, L2, and L3 dimensions .002" short of the reamer drawing.No pressures given so I set the limits the same as 6 ARC AR 15 platform.

From JGS reamer print, no cartridge print supplied.I made the cartridge L1, L2, and L3 dimensions .002" short of the reamer drawing.No pressures given, so I set the limits the same as 6 ARC Bolt platform.
Sized volume was 15.64 grs. H2O.L6 set by maximum the magazine will allow.
H2O capacity based on Peterson 6XC brass necked down. Reamer was  Manson Tubb spec
0.257 projectile in a 6.5 Creedmoor case \u005Cu005Cu005Cu00BCalled 25 Creedmoor

calibers.altname: 257 PRC Precision Rifle Cartridge (Wildcat based on 6.5 PRC)
calibers.descr: A 6.5 PRC necked down to .257 For
Volume as provided by GRT User JordanWilliams.
Transcribed from SAAMI DrawingSince this cartridge lies midscale between 300 PRC and 300 Raum I chose a default Sebert between the two, 6.
Cartridge data from Nosler Book, Chamber is a guess based on 26-06 Rem.Case volume is by GRT estimate.

Sherman ImprovedNotes: fro Shermanneck/shoulder is .003" forward of std. at 40 degreestaper is almost .007" less.throat dimensions are a little differentno neck tapercapacity is 71 grains h20 with Lapua brass

calibers.status: Pending Corrections, manual transcription from SAAMI drawing to CIP.
calibers.descr: R1 dimensions will cause a warning as this is a rebated rim cartridge.The Chamber R1 has to be larger than the Cartridge R1 or the cartridge could not chamber as the entry hole would be smaller than the largest cartridge diemeter.  Ignore this warning.

https://www.berrysmfg.com/product/300-aac-blkout-309-220gr-spBullet O.A.L.: 1.350"Cartridge Name: 300 AAC Blackout Cartridge O.A.L.: 2.150"Max Velocity: 1300 fpsBerry's Superior Plated Bullets are the finest bonded copper-jacketed bullets available today. Starting with a swaged lead core, they are electroplated with copper to their final weight, leaving no lead exposure. They are then re-struck to precise specifications, ensuring a solid bond, and providing consistency with every round. Indoor range safe and unbelievably accurate, Berry's Superior Plated Bullets® are the choice of shooters everywhere.THESE BULLETS ARE NOT RECOMMENDED FOR BARRELS WITH A FASTER TWIST RATE THAN 1:7".
